overall_justification: The newspaper edition primarily reports on events and opinions related to the Convênio de Taubaté and the Caixa de Conversão without taking a definitive stance. It mentions David Campista's modifications to the Convênio and acknowledges potential implications for the exchange rate, but refrains from explicit endorsement or criticism of either the orthodox or expansionist perspectives. The article on the "Quatro de Julho" dominates the front page, relegating Convênio-related news to a secondary role. While the paper reports on market reactions and anxieties surrounding the Convênio, its own editorial voice remains neutral.

supporting_evidence.quote: Foi a nota politica de hontem o parecer do illustre deputado mineiro sobre o Convênio de Taubaté. Hoje publicamos esse parecer na integra, em outro logar. O Sr. David Campista chamou a essa publicação, de que declinou toda a responsabilidade, «indiscreção americana», e declarou que ainda havia modificações a fazer.
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This quote highlights the newspaper's reporting on David Campista's modifications to the Convênio, a key aspect of the debate, but presents it as a factual account rather than taking a position.

supporting_evidence.quote: O cambio funccionou muito sustentado, sem offerta e sem procura. No entanto, corriam noticias alarmantes de Matto Grosso, ao mesmo tempo que se reavivava a questão do Convênio do Café, ora modificado pelo Sr. David Campista.
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This passage demonstrates the newspaper's acknowledgment of market anxieties and the renewed debate surrounding the Convênio, but maintains a neutral tone, simply describing the situation.
